mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for the return difference versus baseline is less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending across durable and non-durable sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in more than 65% of observations during early-cycle phases, or rolling IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market expansion, supporting consumer income growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll changes predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.005 across walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 235.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns, or positive joint growth predicts negative returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines above 1.0% are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the threshold signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
